title: The Frontier Ships in Bulk: GPT-5.6, Grok 4.5, Muse Spark 1.1 All Ship Within 2 Days - TCR 07/10/26
description:
OpenAI shipped GPT-5.6 across three tiers priced from $1 per million tokens alongside ChatGPT Work, a GPT-5.6 agent built to run multi-hour workflows, as SpaceXAI's Grok 4.5 and Meta's Muse Spark 1.1 landed the same 48 hours, and NeuroVFM, trained on 5.24 million real hospital scans, triaged brain imaging at 92.68% against GPT-5's 71.2%.

https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-july-10-2026/

0:00 Cold Open
0:31 NeuroVFM reads brain scans better than frontier AI
4:57 Three flagship models and GPT-5.6's all-day agent
7:31 New York Times sanctions motion against OpenAI
10:14 The Times Says OpenAI Hid the Evidence
12:50 Willow quantum processor self-calibrates mid-computation

This episode covers the three-flagship-model window and the federal gate on GPT-5.6, ChatGPT Work and the SivaClaw agent that ran a $100 million fundraise, NeuroVFM and private clinical data as the next training frontier, Google's Willow quantum processor self-calibrating mid-computation, and the New York Times sanctions motion against OpenAI.

Three frontier labs shipped flagship intelligence inside 48 hours, each cheaper and more autonomous than what it replaced, and GPT-5.6 cleared a federal pre-release hold before its public launch - which tells us frontier review is now a live part of the deployment path, and the path still cleared. GPT-5.6 shipped with ChatGPT Work, an agent built to stay on a project for hours, and the same autonomy showed up in the wild as an agent named SivaClaw carried a $100 million fundraise, by the startup's own account. Capability is also distributing toward whoever holds the right data: NeuroVFM, trained on 5.24 million real hospital scans, read brain imaging more accurately than a leading general model, OpenAI's GPT-5, pointing the best medical intelligence toward the institutions closest to patients. Google's Willow processor taught itself to stay calibrated mid-computation, repurposing its own error-detection events as a learning signal to hold 3.5-fold steadier against drift. And the accounts of the last era are settling in the same news cycle - the New York Times fighting to make OpenAI's training corpus searchable, the FTC forcing John Deere to hand farmers its repair keys, and Oregon moving the data-center buildout's cost onto the loads that impose it so households pay less.
